headline: AST-Bench shows how much database work an AI coding agent pays before the same feature ships.
subhead: Same task. Same Codex run contract. Same acceptance test. MongoDB kept the business state closer to the workflow, so the agent spent less context, less time, and fewer cleanup loops on this verified replay.
proofQuestion: When the same agent builds the same workflow, how much work is product logic and how much is database-state reconstruction?

plainEnglish.headline: The feature shipped in both lanes. MongoDB made the AI read less, wait less, and clean up less.
plainEnglish.seedAnswer: In this verified replay, Codex passed both lanes. Postgres required more context, more elapsed time, and more cleanup signals. MongoDB had the larger code diff, so the page shows that mixed metric instead of hiding it.
plainEnglish.audiencePromise: A CEO sees the business cost. A developer can open the raw trace, diff, tests, database proof, and hashes.

architectureQuestion: Is the real comparison one database versus one database, or one MongoDB data plane versus a stitched stack?

stackComparison.label: MongoDB
stackComparison.summary: Operational data, document shape, full-text search, vector search, and hybrid retrieval can stay close to the application workflow.
stackComparison.proofStatus: Measured here for the operational workflow lane.

stackComparison.label: Postgres + Elastic + Pinecone
stackComparison.summary: A common enterprise stack may split relational state, search, vectors, sync jobs, permissions, and audit proof across multiple systems.
stackComparison.proofStatus: Not measured by this replay; use as the next benchmark question.

currentSeed.label: Seed case study
currentSeed.task: Same enterprise escalation. Same Codex. Same acceptance test. Different schema tax.

comparison.verdict: Codex passed both lanes. Postgres made the AI read 8,869 more estimated tokens, took 70 seconds longer, and showed 4 more cleanup signals. MongoDB changed 1,361 more bytes of code.

processLoop.label: Same request
processLoop.plain: One business request enters both lanes.

processLoop.label: The AI reads
processLoop.plain: It has to understand the app, the data shape, the rules, and the test before it can safely write code.

processLoop.label: The AI tries
processLoop.plain: Every look-around, test run, correction, and retry is captured from the raw Codex trace.

processLoop.label: Code changes
processLoop.plain: Only product source changes count. Test edits and fixture edits invalidate the lane.

processLoop.label: Proof
processLoop.plain: Acceptance, DB before/after, and rendered customer state prove the workflow moved data.

processLoop.label: Cost receipt
processLoop.plain: Measured deltas feed the receipt; sliders only change visible assumptions.

contextLedger.label: Prompt
contextLedger.purpose: Business request given to the agent

contextLedger.label: Run rules
contextLedger.purpose: What the agent was allowed to change

contextLedger.label: Prompt
contextLedger.purpose: Business request given to the agent

contextLedger.label: Run rules
contextLedger.purpose: What the agent was allowed to change

currentSeed.customerCaveat: Single verified replay. Same task, same agent, same acceptance test. Not a promise of savings.
currentSeed.caveat: This seed case is real evidence, but it is not the V1 benchmark result.

fairnessRules: Same business prompt per compared pair.
Same acceptance assertions per compared pair.
Same agent and version per compared pair.
Same repeat count per compared pair.
Database-native models are allowed and required.
Generated fixture records are deterministic and versioned.
No test edits, fixture edits, or human rescue inside an accepted run.
Failures, retries, timeouts, and mixed metrics remain visible.
Cost projections separate measured deltas from assumptions.

evidenceClaims.label: Proof boundary
evidenceClaims.question: What is proven today?
evidenceClaims.verdict: This customer demo proves one verified Codex replay pair. The broader AST-Bench benchmark still requires 450 lane runs; 3 are currently passed.

evidenceClaims.label: Seed Codex traces
evidenceClaims.question: Is the current seed replay real?
evidenceClaims.verdict: Both lanes preserve Codex JSONL/stdout, stderr, elapsed time, and CLI metadata. The Postgres lane consumed 8,869 more estimated transcript tokens in this captured pair.

evidenceClaims.label: Seed code diffs
evidenceClaims.question: What did the agent actually change?
evidenceClaims.verdict: Codex changed 1 MongoDB file(s) and 1 Postgres file(s). MongoDB required 1,361 more diff bytes.

evidenceClaims.label: What the AI had to read
evidenceClaims.question: Which files and context did the agent have to understand?
evidenceClaims.verdict: Seed trace shows 16 MongoDB work events and 23 Postgres work events. The file list is hashable evidence, not a slide.

evidenceClaims.label: One data plane vs stitched stack
evidenceClaims.question: Should the benchmark compare MongoDB only to Postgres, or to Postgres plus search plus vector systems?
evidenceClaims.verdict: The current measured seed is MongoDB versus Postgres. The next benchmark design should add a realistic stitched-stack lane before making any public claim about Elastic or Pinecone.

evidenceClaims.label: Local database proof
evidenceClaims.question: Did the seed evidence touch real local databases?
evidenceClaims.verdict: The proof runners seed real local MongoDB and Postgres services, execute the workflow, and capture before/after workflow state.

evidenceClaims.label: Database-native design review
evidenceClaims.question: Is Postgres being treated credibly?
evidenceClaims.verdict: Every benchmark task requires a database-native design review before public promotion.

evidenceClaims.label: Cost model
evidenceClaims.question: What part is measured and what part is assumed?
evidenceClaims.verdict: Seed projection is $20.4k monthly under visible assumptions. The measured inputs come from the captured replay; monthly scale and review cost are editable assumptions.

evidenceClaims.label: Anti-cheat gates
evidenceClaims.question: Can the page overclaim?
evidenceClaims.verdict: The benchmark gates block public-v1 labels without the required run count and evidence files.

costModel.publicLabel: $20.4k projected monthly impact under your assumptions
costModel.caveat: Projection uses measured replay deltas plus visible assumptions. It is not a promise of savings.

metricDefinitions.label: What the AI had to read
metricDefinitions.plain: Estimated context in the captured agent transcript. More reading means more model budget and more chances to misunderstand the system.

metricDefinitions.label: Human babysitting pressure
metricDefinitions.plain: Signals like error, failed, retry, fix, and fail in the captured run. It is a proxy for cleanup pressure, not a correctness score by itself.

metricDefinitions.label: Review rate / hour
metricDefinitions.plain: The loaded hourly cost of a person checking AI output. It is a visible assumption slider, not a measured runtime fact.

metricDefinitions.label: Code changed
metricDefinitions.plain: Bytes changed in the accepted source diff. This is the mixed metric: MongoDB changed more code in this run, so the page shows it openly.

strategy.nextV1Move: Run the full 25 task x 2 database lane x 3 agent x 3 repeat matrix, then publish only aggregate measured results.

stressLadder.label: V1 benchmark
stressLadder.publicClaim: Seed/case-study until every required lane is captured.

stressLadder.label: 1,000 object stress tier
stressLadder.scale: 1,000 tables or collections, migration/history files, schema docs, and noisy legacy modules
stressLadder.publicClaim: Designed to test whether the effect grows with enterprise complexity.

reproduction.label: Run one Codex seed-style cell
reproduction.command: npm run benchmark:run -- --task strategic-account-rescue --lane mongo --agent codex --repeat 1

caveats: Current public data is seed case-study evidence until the 450 required V1 lane runs are captured.
AST-Bench V1 requires Codex, Claude Code, and Cursor before multi-agent claims are allowed.
Cost projection separates captured run deltas from user-editable assumptions.
Mixed metrics remain visible; a MongoDB loss on any metric is not hidden.
